recommendation_templates.department: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory
praise_phrases: one of the most intellectually curious students I have encountered in my 15 years of teaching
demonstrates exceptional analytical rigor and creativity in problem-solving
possesses a rare blend of technical mastery and collaborative spirit
their research contributions were pivotal to the project's success
consistently produced work that was not only correct but elegantly designed
academic_achievements: authored a conference paper on federated learning privacy, accepted to NeurIPS
achieved the highest score in the graduate-level Advanced Algorithms course
developed a novel optimization algorithm that improved model training efficiency by 40%
led a team of four to win the department's annual research showcase
consistently ranked in the top 1% of all undergraduate students in the department

praise_phrases: exhibits a profound depth of understanding that is uncommon at the undergraduate level
approach to experimental design is both meticulous and innovative
has an innate ability to identify the core challenge in a complex biological system
their written and oral communication skills are outstanding
a natural leader who elevates the work of everyone around them
academic_achievements: co-first author on a paper in 'Nature Biomedical Engineering' on CRISPR delivery systems
designed and 3D-printed a microfluidic device for single-cell analysis
received the university's prestigious Goldwater Scholarship
presented independent research at the annual BMES conference, winning the best poster award
maintained a perfect 4.0 GPA while conducting 20+ hours of research per week

praise_phrases: possesses an extraordinary talent for econometric modeling and causal inference
their theoretical insights are consistently complemented by rigorous empirical validation
shows a mature grasp of complex economic literature and its real-world implications
arguments are always logically structured, persuasive, and elegantly communicated
I rank them among the top two or three students I have taught in the past decade
academic_achievements: thesis on inequality and automation was awarded the department's 'Best Undergraduate Thesis' prize
developed a new statistical method to correct for bias in survey data, now used in a ongoing government study
published a solo-authored critique in the 'Cambridge Journal of Economics'
achieved the highest mark in the challenging Tripos Part IIB examinations
secured a competitive research assistantship with the Centre for Economic Performance

recommendation_templates.department: Division of Physics, Mathematics and Astronomy
praise_phrases: mathematical intuition is nothing short of remarkable
tackles problems in theoretical physics with a unique and powerful geometric perspective
ability to absorb advanced concepts and immediately apply them in new contexts is exceptional
their dedication to foundational understanding is evident in every discussion
I have no doubt they are destined to make significant contributions to fundamental science
academic_achievements: solved an open problem in classical mechanics related to non-holonomic constraints, resulting in a preprint on arXiv
scored a perfect score on the notoriously difficult Physics Graduate Qualifying Exam
contributed derivations to our research group's paper on quantum gravity, now under review at PRL
taught supplemental instruction sessions for graduate-level Quantum Field Theory, receiving stellar reviews
was selected as a finalist for the national Department of Energy SCGSR fellowship

praise_phrases: analytical writing is of publishable quality, displaying both clarity and profound critical thought
has an unparalleled ability to synthesize historical context with contemporary political theory
their questions in seminar often reframe the debate and lead to richer discussions
shows a nuanced understanding of institutional design and political behavior
is the kind of scholar who will define the next generation of political science research
academic_achievements: undergraduate thesis on democratic backsliding was nominated for the Samuel P. Huntington Award
conducted original archival research in three countries for a study on post-conflict constitutions
published an op-ed analysis in 'The Washington Post' based on their course research
served as head research assistant for a multi-year NSF-funded project on voter mobilization
graduated summa cum laude with the highest honors in the Government department

recommendation_templates.department: Department of Mechanical and Process Engineering
praise_phrases: combines brilliant engineering design with an unwavering commitment to sustainable solutions
their prototypes are not only functional but exhibit manufacturing elegance and cost-awareness
demonstrates mastery across the full spectrum from simulation (FEA/CFD) to hands-on lab fabrication
a pragmatic problem-solver who never loses sight of the broader systemic impact
leadership in group projects is characterized by clear vision and empowering mentorship
academic_achievements: led a student team to design a winning low-cost water purification device in an international engineering competition
filed a provisional patent for a novel heat exchanger design with 30% improved efficiency
their master's thesis on battery thermal management was sponsored by a leading automotive company
optimized a combustion process in our lab, reducing particulate emissions by 25% in experimental trials
consistently ranked first in a cohort of over 200 highly competitive students

praise_phrases: philological skills are exceptional, with a command of both Greek and Latin that rivals doctoral students
their textual interpretations are insightful, original, and meticulously argued
possesses a historian's sensitivity to context and a literary scholar's eye for narrative form
writing is not only erudite but also a genuine pleasure to read
I consider them one of the most promising young classicists to emerge from Oxford in recent years
academic_achievements: deciphered and proposed a new reading for a fragmentary papyrus text, a finding presented at the SCS annual meeting
awarded the Chancellor's Prize for the best performance in Classical Honour Moderations
translated and annotated a previously unstudied set of medieval scholia on Sophocles
published a peer-reviewed article in 'Classical Quarterly' while still an undergraduate
was elected President of the Oxford University Classical Society

praise_phrases: a programming virtuoso with an exceptional ability to architect complex, scalable software systems
their code is a model of clarity, efficiency, and robust error handling
understands the human-computer interaction aspects of systems design as deeply as the technical ones
consistently identifies edge cases and security implications that others overlook
has the rare potential to become a leader in both industrial research and advanced development
academic_achievements: won the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC) regional championship as team lead
contributed significant code to a major open-source distributed systems project (Apache Kafka)
designed and taught a student-run course on advanced systems programming that had a waitlist of 100+
interned at a top tech firm where their performance review ranked them in the top 1% of all interns
developed a new file system for educational use that is now deployed in the department's undergraduate labs
